info.description: If you're creating your own crypto service, bot, or working with a large number of addresses, automation is key. For these types of tasks, we've developed an API integration with re:Fee—connect and manage your wallet energy using simple and reliable requests.
info.title: re:Fee API Documentation

post.description:
Adding a wallet to Always Charged.

Energy is restored when at least **5%** of the initial energy level has been consumed.

The current energy level and price can be obtained by requesting `/api/always_charged/tariffs`.

The service is charged at the rate specified daily, and energy is not recharged.

400.description: The wallet does not exist or is not activated

401.description: Invalid API key or it was not transmitted

403.description: No access rights / IP is not whitelisted

409.description: The address is already connected to the service

post.description:
Adding a wallet to Auto Charging.

`resource`: Resource type. Available: `energy`, `bandwidth`.

`base_resource`: One-time resource charge for 3 days, can be set to 0.

`min_resource`: Minimum resource level in the wallet before a refill occurs.

`refill_resource`: Refills resource for 1 hour.


Prices for this service can be obtained by requesting `/api/auto_charging/tariffs`.

post.description:
Purchasing a resource for a specified period. Available periods and prices can be found by requesting /api/rent_resource/tariffs.

The resource is returned after the period expires, and it will also be fully refunded if 98% of the original capacity is used.

Limits: 32,000 <= 3,000,000, 1,000 <= 50,000
**resource**: energy, bandwidth
